title: USTR Initiates Section 301 Probe into 15 Countries for Excess Capacity
detail: On March 11, 2026, the US Trade Representative initiated Section 301 investigations into structural excess manufacturing capacity across 15 countries and regions, including China, the EU, Japan, India, and Mexico. These investigations could lead to the re-imposition of IEEPA tariffs. The move follows the February 20 executive order imposing a 10% blanket tariff on all countries under Section 122.

title: VP Vance Heads Second DOGE Task Force on Government Fraud
detail: Vice President JD Vance heads a new executive task force in March 2026 cracking down on government fraud, representing the second major DOGE initiative. Mixed economic results: private employers cited 'DOGE impact' for 63,583 layoffs; the US economy lost 92,000 jobs in February with unemployment rising to 4.4%. Federal deficit through November FY2026 is at $439 billion — 19% lower than the prior year due to tariff revenues and reduced public spending.

title: DOGE Cuts Hampering Government Operations During Iran War
detail: Reports from March 2026 indicate that DOGE budget and staffing cuts are hampering US government operations during the ongoing Iran conflict. The intersection of aggressive government workforce reductions and wartime operational requirements has created friction, with some agencies struggling to maintain full capacity while managing concurrent military and diplomatic requirements.
